peptides.name: BPC-157
peptides.research: Over 120 peer-reviewed studies demonstrate accelerated healing via the nitric oxide pathway. Originally derived from human gastric juice, BPC-157 shows remarkable tissue repair properties across tendon, ligament, muscle, and intestinal tissue.
peptides.mechanism: BPC-157 activates the VEGF pathway, enhancing nitric oxide synthesis and angiogenesis. It upregulates growth hormone receptors and promotes tendon cell proliferation.
pros: Accelerates tendon and ligament healing by 30-40%
No known significant side effects at therapeutic doses
Systemic and local effects depending on injection site
cons: Limited human clinical trials (mostly animal studies)
Must source from reputable suppliers (quality varies)
Optimal dosing protocols still being established
warnings: Cancer: theoretical concern with any growth factor - consult physician
Blood pressure: may lower BP (caution with hypotension)
Blood thinners: may interact with anticoagulants
inst: Subcutaneous injection near injury site OR oral
Morning injection on empty stomach optimal
Rotate injection sites to prevent irritation

peptides.name: TB-500
peptides.research: TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4) is a naturally occurring peptide present in virtually all human cells. Clinical trials for wound healing and tissue repair. Shown to promote cell migration, proliferation, and differentiation.
peptides.mechanism: TB-500 regulates actin polymerization, which is essential for cell structure and movement. It promotes angiogenesis, reduces inflammation, and enhances cell migration to injury sites.
pros: Promotes cell migration and tissue repair
No known toxicity at therapeutic doses
cons: Limited human clinical data (mostly animal studies)
warnings: Cancer: may theoretically promote cell proliferation
General: start with lower doses to assess tolerance
inst: Can combine with BPC-157 for enhanced healing
Best used during recovery from injury/surgery

peptides.name: Ipamorelin
peptides.research: Ipamorelin is a selective GH secretagogue that stimulates the pituitary gland to release growth hormone. Unlike other GHRPs, it does not significantly raise cortisol or prolactin levels.
peptides.mechanism: Ipamorelin binds to ghrelin receptors in the pituitary, triggering GH release without affecting other hormones. It maintains the body's natural negative feedback mechanisms.
pros: Can be used long-term without losing effectiveness
cons: Must be taken on empty stomach
inst: Wait 30 minutes after injection before eating

peptides.name: Semaglutide
peptides.research: FDA-approved GLP-1 receptor agonist (Ozempic, Wegovy). Clinical trials show 15-20% body weight reduction over 68 weeks. Also approved for type 2 diabetes management.
peptides.mechanism: Semaglutide mimics GLP-1 hormone, slowing gastric emptying, increasing insulin secretion, and reducing appetite via hypothalamic signaling.
cons: Nausea and GI side effects common
Muscle loss possible with rapid weight loss
warnings: Thyroid C-cell tumors: contraindicated with personal/family history
Pregnancy: discontinue at least 2 months before
inst: Start at 0.25mg weekly, titrate every 4 weeks
Combine with diet and exercise for best results

peptides.name: Tirzepatide
peptides.research: FDA-approved (Mounjaro/Zepbound) dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist. Clinical trials show up to 22.5% body weight reduction - the highest of any approved weight loss medication.
peptides.mechanism: Tirzepatide activates both GIP and GLP-1 receptors, providing enhanced appetite suppression, improved insulin sensitivity, and greater weight loss than GLP-1 agonists alone.
cons: More GI side effects than semaglutide
warnings: Hypoglycemia: risk when combined with insulin/sulfonylureas
inst: Start at 2.5mg weekly, increase every 4 weeks
Take on same day each week
Must titrate slowly to minimize GI effects
